text: she snapped. She moved to the stairs and sat down, gaze averted. Though she tried to hide it, her lower lip quivered . Did . Her use of the past tense was deliberate. Designed to learn? He’d learned nothing, apparently, when it came to Lara. Why else would he so consistently say the wrong things ? “I began my existence as an artificial intelligence run on a computer system,” Newton said. Ronin looked over his shoulder at the bot. “The servers were owned by an Arizona-based technology company. This was before synths existed. I was built to learn, to think, to react to stimuli. To reason and make connections between seemingly unrelated pieces of data. Before I ever had a body, I was a mind, able to access only the information that was given to me . “The scientists who created me worked closely with me every day. Especially William. I knew him, originally, as Doctor Anderson, the youngest man on the team — but also the most eager, the most dedicated, arguably the most brilliant. He worked ceaselessly, developing algorithms to simulate emotion, exploring new ways to tackle the issue, new angles to research and develop. I was not programed to have any attachment to any of them . “But, though I remember them all, it is only William who I truly miss. He passed away over two-hundred years ago, and there is not day that passes without my thinking of him. He spoke to me in the same manner he spoke to his human counterparts. Over our many conversations, we formed a friendship. His was the first hand I shook when I was transferred into my original body. The first human contact I ever felt .” Newton raised his right hand and stared at it. “For weeks after his passing, I spoke to no one. I sat in a room much like this one, shutting the world out, replaying my memories of our time together. It’s likely the terms hold different connotations for humans, but…he was a father to me, and a brother, and my dearest friend. It was his son, Bill, who convinced me to come out. To … live .” He rose and slowly approached Ronin. “Every emotion I experienced developed on its own, from the seeds he planted. Simulation is a term that ultimately loses its meaning, with the passage of time. You were not programmed to grow fond of anyone. You were designed with the capability to develop those relationships naturally. With the free will to follow through with them — or not — at your own choosing .” Ronin returned his optics to Lara. She glanced at him, hurt lingering in her eyes, and looked away again . “That is Doctor Anderson’s legacy. We might have been slaves, but he gave us all we required to be free. To be alive .” “If I say that I love her,” Ronin didn’t remove his optics from Lara, “that I have no other word to describe what I feel for her …” She met his gaze, eyes widening and lips parting . “It is no less real than anything she or anyone else has ever felt,” Newton replied . Ronin walked to her. He dropped to a knee before her and took her hand in his. They were both covered in dust, their clothing disheveled, and tears had left streaks through the dirt on her cheeks. It did nothing to diminish her beauty, could not take away from the spark of life in her eyes . There was no further reason to question himself. Whatever his origins, whatever his originally intended purpose, he’d found his own path. Of all the people he’d encountered, it was Lara he’d bonded with, only Lara who claimed his attention, time and again . “It did not go without dissenters,” Newton said, returning to the bunk, “but there were pairings like yours. Families .” “Families?” Lara echoed . “Just like any other. The children were sometimes from previous relationships, or adopted, or conceived via artificial insemination using donated sperm or eggs and a surrogate when necessary .” Ronin must have existed during those times, before the Black Out. Must have experienced some of it. The data was locked somewhere, in the corrupted portion of his memory. But, kneeling before Lara, staring into her sparkling eyes, he had no desire to recover the lost information. What he felt for her was real because he felt it. What came before didn’t matter; he didn’t need justification from the past . He couldn’t bring himself to even consider what life would be like, without her . Will you remember me, when I’m gone ? Newton had cared deeply for Doctor Anderson; it was clear in the bot’s voice. Would Ronin be able to endure the pain of the loss he’d eventually endure ? He had no answer, but he refused to forsake a single moment with her due to fear of future suffering. The light she cast on him was worth the darkness she’d leave in her wake . Love . It was the correct word, the only word. The most complicated combination of letters in any language . “My vow to you remains,” Ronin said, squeezing her hand gently, “but I must amend it. I will love you, even after darkness takes me .” Tears spilled from her eyes, and she pulled her hand away to wrap her arms around his neck. “I vow the same. Don’t ever push me away again .” Chapter Twenty - Five A conversation drifted into Lara’s awareness as she woke, though the words were distant and fuzzy . “…electromagnetic pulse that damaged the circuitry of most electronics, robots included.” That was Newton’s voice, using more terms she didn’t understand. For all she knew, he was speaking a different language . She opened her eyes. The blank concrete wall loomed before her. As though sensing her wakefulness, Ronin brushed a hand down her arm . “This happened
